# bitchat

> On-device voice chat CLI for macOS 26+ on Apple Silicon. ASR + LLM + TTS
> all run locally via mlx-swift; no network calls, no API keys. Built and
> maintained by bitHuman (https://www.bithuman.ai).

`bitchat` is a Homebrew-installable command-line tool. A user running it
can have a real-time spoken conversation with a local LLM, with voice
cloning from a short audio clip. Designed to feel like cloud voice
assistants but stay 100% on-device.

## Install

```
brew tap bithuman/bitchat https://github.com/bithuman-product/bitchat
brew install bitchat
```

Requires macOS 26 (Tahoe)+, Apple Silicon (M1+), ≥ 8 GB RAM, ~3 GB free
disk for model weights (cached at `~/.cache/huggingface/hub/` after the
first launch).

## Run

```
bitchat                                # default English voice
bitchat --locale ja-JP                 # Japanese ASR + TTS
bitchat --voice Aiden                  # English preset speaker
bitchat --voice ~/voices/me.wav        # clone a voice from a 10–20 s clip
bitchat --prompt "Be terse."           # override system prompt
bitchat --prompt @/path/to/persona.txt # … or read it from a file
bitchat --help                         # full usage
```

Built-in TTS preset speakers: English (`Ryan`, `Aiden`); Chinese
(`Vivian`, `Serena`, `Uncle_Fu`, `Dylan`, `Eric`).

Cancel the bot's reply at any point by speaking — barge-in cutoff is
under 80 ms end-to-end. Quit with Ctrl-C.

## Architecture (high-level)

| layer | implementation |
|---|---|
| ASR | Apple SpeechAnalyzer (`Speech` framework, on-device, macOS 26+) |
| LLM | `mlx-community/gemma-4-E2B-it-4bit` via `ml-explore/mlx-swift-lm` |
| TTS | `mlx-community/Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-0.6B-Base-4bit` via `Blaizzy/mlx-audio-swift` |
| AEC | hardware: `AVAudioEngine` voice-processing IO unit on both ends |
| Voice cloning | in-context speaker conditioning via Qwen3-TTS `refAudio` + `refText` |

Source code (Swift 6, SPM) lives in a private dev repo
(`bithuman-product/bitchat-dev`). External installs come through this
public repo's Homebrew formula and notarised release zips.

## Library use (Swift)

Embedding bitchat as a library (`BitChatKit` SwiftPM target) is
supported but currently invitation-only. The public API surface looks
like:

```swift
import BitChatKit

var config = VoiceChatConfig()
config.localeIdentifier = "en-US"
config.systemPrompt = "You are a deadpan ship's computer. One sentence."
config.voice = .preset("Aiden")
// or .clone(referenceAudio: URL, transcript: String)
// or .default

let chat = VoiceChat(config: config)
try await chat.start()
```

Open an issue at https://github.com/bithuman-product/bitchat/issues if
you'd like access to the source repo for library embedding.
